summary: Tested Google OAuth integration alongside existing JWT auth. All 8 backend pytest cases pass and all 5 frontend flows verified via Playwright. JWT auth continues to work (no regression). Google OAuth session endpoint properly rejects invalid session IDs. Simulated Google session_token authenticates users to /api/auth/me and protected streaming endpoints. Logout deletes DB session and clears cookies. Frontend correctly redirects to auth.emergentagent.com, renders AuthCallback view on session_id hash, and redirects back to /login on failure.
critical_code_review_comments: get_current_user session_token fallback via Authorization: Bearer heuristic (dot-count) works but is fragile — consider a dedicated header or explicit prefix.

POST /api/auth/session sets cookie with samesite='none' + secure=True while JWT cookies use samesite='lax' + secure=False. This inconsistency is fine over HTTPS in preview but should be unified for prod.

In get_current_user the Google branch mutates user['_id'] = user_id but downstream code (e.g. mood/verse save) stores user['_id'] into user_id — mixing JWT ObjectId strings and Google user_ids in the same collection field is acceptable but not documented.
backend/server.py is 800+ lines — should be split into auth/, bible/, ai/ modules per the >700 line guideline.
test_credentials: admin@biblebio.com / BibleAdmin2024! (JWT). Google session simulated via MongoDB user_sessions insert.
seed_data_creation: Created ephemeral test user_id 'user_google_test_*' + session_token in MongoDB during pytest run; cleaned up in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Google OAuth cannot be tested end-to-end without a real session_id from auth.emergentagent.com. Use MongoDB user_sessions collection to simulate. JWT auth path uses ObjectId in _id; Google path uses string user_id. Both work with get_current_user helper.
